title: RTP / RTCP
description: Real-time Transport Protocol — delivers audio and video over UDP for VoIP, video conferencing, and streaming.
fullExplanation: RTP (Real-time Transport Protocol) provides end-to-end delivery of real-time data (audio, video) over UDP. It adds sequence numbers (for reordering), timestamps (for synchronization and jitter calculation), and payload type identification. RTCP (RTP Control Protocol) runs alongside RTP — it carries QoS statistics (packet loss, jitter, RTT) between participants. SIP (Session Initiation Protocol) or WebRTC use RTP for the actual media stream after signaling.

fields.name: Padding (P)
fields.description: Padding bytes at end of packet

fields.name: CC
fields.description: CSRC count — number of contributing sources

fields.name: Sequence Number
fields.description: Increments per packet — detect loss and reorder

fields.name: Timestamp
fields.description: Sampling instant — used for playout timing and jitter

fields.name: SSRC
fields.description: Synchronization Source — unique ID for this stream

keyFacts: RTP uses even UDP ports, RTCP uses the next odd port (e.g. RTP=5004, RTCP=5005)
RTP does NOT guarantee delivery or ordering — application handles jitter buffer
Jitter buffer: absorbs timing variations — typically 50-200ms for VoIP
RTCP SR (Sender Report): statistics from sender — packets sent, bytes sent, NTP timestamp
RTCP RR (Receiver Report): statistics from receiver — fraction lost, jitter, last SR
SRTP (Secure RTP): adds AES encryption and HMAC authentication to RTP
WebRTC uses SRTP mandatorily — all browser-based video/audio is encrypted
Codecs: G.711 (PSTN quality), G.729 (compressed), Opus (modern, adaptive)
